title: Level 386
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 386:''' "Skyward Ladders"}}{{Blurbackground|file=MawMap.jpg}} {{Glacier Theme}}{{Class 0|color-h=-160|hexagon=d|safety=Safe|security=Secure|entity=Dream-Related Hazards|class=1d - Dreamful|header=Threat Index: Class}} '''Level 386''', popularly known as "Skyward Ladders" or "Ladder Zone" is the 387th level of [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]]. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=MawMap.jpg|align=right|caption=One of the only available images of '''Level 386'''.}} '''Level 386''' is an expansive and quickly moving sky, with different layers of clouds. These clouds appear to vary in color and shape, with some having a defined, recognizable figure, like landscapes or animals. This is because the wind on the level is nearly unpredictable. It redirects parts of clouds quickly into recognizable shapes. Across '''Level 386''', there are anomalous basement ladders, which extend miles downward, into multiple subsequent basements. Around these ladders, are invisible walls and floor, which keep wanderers from falling eternally. These walls are unbreakable, the only way to pass through them is by no-clipping. '''Level 386''' appears to visually spin around a wanderer at a relatively fast speed, although it may vary. This may cause nausea or vomiting. A wanderer should close their eyes or find shelter in basements, which remain unaffected. The aforementioned basements on '''Level 386''' do not follow the natural laws of physics; from the outside, are invisible, only the ladders leading to them can be seen. However, from within, they are ordinary basements, with old machines, boxes of supplies, and cobwebs. As one plunges into the spiral of seemingly endless basements within '''Level 386''', one will notice the ever-growing presence of windows, which grow more and more, and appear in higher quantities as one passes through floors of basements. The final floor of the basements is named Finalidae. It is not known how long it takes to reach Finalidae, the floor's location appears to alter from wanderer to wanderer. In this area, there are no walls or ceilings, only ground. Regardless, anomalies may cause floating windows and ceilings to materialize. Smashed crates filled with expired [[Object 1|Almond Water]], ripped cobwebs, and fragments of machines, are common objects found in Finalidae. After one enters Finalidae, it is impossible to exit via any means other than jumping off, leading one to [[the Void]]. It is also thought that arriving at Finalidae may cause a traveler to gradually fade into [[Level 387]], and the last they will see of '''Level 386''' is a stone statue, pondering at the endless sky. {{Blurimage|file=Sky Anomaly.jpg|caption=A sky anomaly.|align=left}} Some clouds have varying anomalies and properties based on their colors. Clouds with warm colors will be more humid, and hot, some may even melt certain materials on contact. Instances with colder hues are so cold, that sometimes parts of these clouds become ice, staying close to cold clouds may result in hypothermia or frostbite. Both of these clouds cannot produce common rain. Most water in warm clouds becomes vapor as soon as it comes in contact with it, and the frigid ones are too icy, to the point where rain is composed of snowflakes. '''Level 386''' contains a set of odd anomalies. For instance, '''Level 386''' appears to suffer from a subtle variation of [[Phenomenon 15|Alone]]. Instead of copying the entire level, only a small part is copied, which is known as "Fraction". Strangely, these copies appear to all retain subtle differences. At specific periods, the sky suffers from anomalies, like copying, color changing, nighttime, etc. These anomalies are rare and purely visual, they do not affect wanderers physically or mentally in any manner. A noticeable effect is the 386 effect, its symptoms will be listed below: {| class="fandom-table" |+The 386 Effect; symptoms (Revision of 2024.) !Time !Symptom !Symptom !Symptom |- |30 minutes |Relaxation |Calming sensation |Déjà Vu |- |Two hours |Intense nostalgia |Increased happiness and brainpower |Sloth and lack of motivation |- |Four months |Minor hallucinations |Epiphany |Longer resting |} Although '''Level 386''' lacks common hazards, like entities, it has dream-related hazards. On '''Level 386''', dreams gain enhanced realism. This triggers many effects, like a wanderer's enhanced memory of the dream, which may last months on end; the impact the dream has; inducing of hallucinations described as "the most real the false can be"; etc. On extreme cases, dreams may become so realistic, that a person may be mentally influenced by it, a dream may convince a traveler of commiting murders and other crimes. '''Level 386''' is one of the levels created by the 380s 1976 [[Splits|Split]]. As a result, it suffers from several effects [[Level 385]] and [[Level 387]] also suffer. Out of the 380s, '''Level 386''' appears to be the most affected, it is more mentally unsafe and unstable. The most noticeable of these outcomes is the Contact Zone, an area located where is believed to be the north of '''Level 386'''. The Contact Zone, much like [[Level 387|the Outer Side]] or [[Level 385|the Altures]], is heavily unstable. It violates laws of physics, and lacks gravity in most of its expanse. The Contact Zone retains what one would call portals, which sometimes teleport structures or even wanderers in [[Level 385]] and [[Level 387|387]] to this location. No-clipping is very prominent and gravitational anomalies are typical. Unlike in [[Level 387]], gravity, rather than being low, does not happen within the Contact Zone. Due to this, traversing on the Contact Zone is infeasible. Contrary to [[Level 387]], a wrong move cannot be undone by grabbing onto a floating structure, and the traveler will remain endlessly floating around the region. == Sentient Presence == [[Level 385|Fake Birds]] and [[Kats]] are most common entities found within '''Level 386'''. Rumors speak of two enigmatic entities that inhabit this level, these entities being The Dust Collector, and the Cloud People. ===The Dust Collector=== {{Entity Class|Agg=1|Int=4|Dan=0|Hab=Level 386|no=UND.}}{{Blurimage|file=The Dust Collector.png|align=right|hovertext=I've arrived.|caption=A witness sketch of The Dust Collector.|bg-align=left|width=250px}} The Dust Collector is an individual entity encountered within '''Level 386'''. It is a shapeshifting entity made of ash and dust, and white, glowing eyes, according to witnesses. The Dust Collector affects the spaces around it, creating a dark aura, which absorbs light and creates an effect similar to television static. It appears to clean the basements and consumes dust. Wanderers report that The Dust Collector stalks them, and when noticed, disappears. It is thought by residents that The Dust Collector is made up of the same ash and dust it consumes, however, it is yet to be confirmed. ===The Cloud People=== {{Entity Class|Agg=0|Int=1|Dan=0|Hab=Level 386|no=UND.}} The Cloud People are enigmatic entities within '''Level 386''', which one will likely encounter during their venture down the basements. These entities receive their names from their cloud-like humanoid appearance. They present odd behavior, which does not appear to occur with any other entity on this level. They are notorious for soullessly staring into the unending expanse of the level's sky, the clouds moving, the rain happening, and the moon coming in. The Cloud People are hypnotized by the sky, observing it for hours, appreciating its hidden beauty. These entities typically die from hunger, because they cannot leave by themselves. == Human Settlements == Due to the level's isolation effect, it is impossible to form human outposts. == Entrances and Exits == ===Entrances=== * Staying within [[Level 387|the Outer Side]] in [[Level 387]] or [[Level 385|the Altures]] in [[Level 385]]. * If one wanders too far off on [[Level 993.1]], they may accidentally reach this level. * Old-looking skyscrapers on [[Level 97]] may lead here. * Theories suggest that certain actions in the sea of [[Level 413]] lead here. * ===Exits=== *Staying within the Contact Zone may lead into [[Level 385]] or [[Level 387]]. *It is said that no-clipping into certain boxes in the basements will lead to [[Level 993]]. *Breaking through the windows on the basements will lead to [[Level 264]]. *Jumping off will lead to [[the Void]]. {{Redirect|Prev=385|Next=387}}{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
